channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Exploring electron spin dynamics in spin chains using defects as a quantum probe
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 17 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70589-8

This study reveals how quantum spin chain edge states-topological defects in organic magnetic materials-maintain quantum coherence. Researchers found that strong internal correlations within these many-body systems suppress dipolar interactions, extending coherence times beyond isolated spins at equivalent concentrations.
item.dc:title: Exploring electron spin dynamics in spin chains using defects as a quantum probe
item.dc:creator: Loic Soriano
Achuthan Manoj Kumar
Guillaume Gerbaud
Adrien Savoyant
Remy Dassonneville
Hervé Vezin
Olivier Jeannin
Maylis Orio
Marc Fourmigué
Sylvain Bertaina

item.title: Heat-assisted hot-hole transfer increases the surface-enhanced Raman activity of Au-TiO<sub>2</sub> nanoarrays
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 17 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70822-4

SERS analysis at higher temperatures, such as during photothermal processes, is challenging due to enhancement losses. Here, the authors demonstrate a heat-induced increase in SERS activity mechanistically attributed to assisted hot-hole transfer.
item.dc:title: Heat-assisted hot-hole transfer increases the surface-enhanced Raman activity of Au-TiO<sub>2</sub> nanoarrays
item.dc:creator: Mengya Zhang
Tongcheng Yu
Hao Liu
Chao Lin
Yaping Yang
Bowen Lv
Qi Zhang
Ming Chen
Tianshuai Wang
Weihong Hua
Kai Han
